Respiratory tract, bronchial brushing or lung FNA specimen
PSC Category VI: Malignant — pulmonary adenocarcinoma

Three-dimensional acinar, papillary, or glandular clusters show enlarged eccentric nuclei, irregular membranes, fine-to-coarse chromatin, prominent nucleoli, high N:C ratio, and vacuolated mucinous cytoplasm.
Necrotic or mucinous background; TTF-1, napsin A, CK7 positive; p40/CK5/6 negative; molecular testing preserved.
Intracytoplasmic mucin with glandular acini distinguishes this vs Squamous cell carcinoma, which has keratinization, bridges, and p40 positivity.
Serous cavity, peritoneal or pleural effusion specimen
TIS Category V: MAL-S — metastatic high-grade serous carcinoma

Papillary fragments, spheroids, and single cells show marked pleomorphic nuclei, coarse chromatin, prominent nucleoli, high mitotic/apoptotic activity, high N:C ratio, and dense or vacuolated cytoplasm.
Hemorrhagic proteinaceous effusion, psammoma bodies possible; PAX8, WT1, ER positive; p53 aberrant; calretinin negative.
PAX8/WT1 positivity with aberrant p53 distinguishes this vs Reactive mesothelial proliferation, which retains calretinin and wild-type p53.
